_comment: MARCHLAND scenario: Cannae, 216 BC. The capstone officer-AI + meaning-layer entry.
_thesis: The frontage cap IS the envelopment. The controlled withdrawal of the center is a COMMAND DECISION (player or officer-AI), never a script. The Iberian/Gallic center must hold the MEANING of 'give ground as ordered' against its own reading of 'we are losing' (Addendum X) or the withdrawal decays into a rout and the trap never closes.
_receipts_note: No quality coefficients. Carthaginian veteran cohorts have high drill-days (receipts), African flanks are fresh, cavalry superiority is a numbers+horse-quality receipt. Roman deep deployment is the historical BLUNDER, encoded as geometry (too dense to bring depth to bear under the frontage cap), not a debuff.

cohorts._role: Roman mass — deep, dense, frontage-capped (the blunder as geometry)
cohorts._note: 8 maniples deep where 3 would fight; rear ranks press, cannot engage

cohorts._role: Carthaginian/Iberian center — the bowed line that must give ground WITHOUT breaking
cohorts._note: disc 0.8 + the withdrawal repertoire + the meaning institution are what let it bend not break

cohorts._role: African veterans, left flank — fresh, in column, wait to wheel inward
African veterans, right flank — fresh, in column, wait to wheel inward
Carthaginian heavy cavalry — wins its wing and RETURNS to hit the rear
cohorts._note: the return is the kill; cavalry that pursues off-map loses Cannae

ordered_retreat_holds._transform_note: the center reads its own dead and the pressure as the EXPECTED PRICE of a planned maneuver, not as defeat — cue values attenuated before the universal threshold
ordered_retreat_holds._break_note: if Mago falls or the cohort's reading flips, the withdrawal becomes a real rout and the Roman mass pours through the gap — the trap fails

officer_test._what_this_grades: Can the AI center execute 'give ground without breaking', and does the meaning hold?
officer_test.subject: mago_center

pass_conditions.withdrawal_executed: center gives ground in good order in the majority of seeds (does not rout on contact)
pass_conditions.meaning_holds_until_flanks_commit: ordered_retreat_holds institution survives until the African flanks wheel (sentiment:we_are_losing stays < 0.6 through the bend)
pass_conditions.envelopment_closes: with withdrawal + wheel + cavalry return, Roman mass is enveloped and its appraisal cascade fires from the OUTSIDE IN (behind-cue spike on the outer ranks first)
pass_conditions.kill_concentration: Roman dead concentrate AFTER encirclement closes, not during the bend (percolation, not attrition)

reject_conditions.scripted_withdrawal: FAIL if the center withdraws on a timer regardless of belief/meaning (that is a script, not a decision)
reject_conditions.rout_on_contact: FAIL if the center breaks the instant it takes pressure (withdrawal repertoire absent or meaning not applied)
reject_conditions.trace_cheat: FAIL if any officer acts on enemy positions outside its belief DB

counterfactual_kill_mago._setup: same scenario, mago_center killed at the moment of first contact
counterfactual_kill_mago.expect: ordered_retreat_holds fails (failure_condition met) -> center reads raw cues -> withdrawal becomes rout -> Roman mass penetrates -> envelopment does NOT close -> Roman victory or draw in the majority of seeds
counterfactual_kill_mago._teaches: the genius was the system (right troops, right meaning, right officer), not the trick — killing the keeper of the meaning loses Cannae

_pursuit_note: Cannae's kill-share is enormous BECAUSE the encircled have nowhere to flee — this scenario is also a stress test for the converging-horn pursuit fix (M7.0), the same open miss as Isandlwana's defender_dead_frac
